raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "asserts a causal psychosomatic link to frame mental patterns as direct somatic influencers",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "thoughts possess intrinsic 'sickly' or health-promoting qualities",
    "mental states reliably produce corresponding bodily states",
    "somatic symptoms can be traced to cognitive origins",
    "altering thoughts will alter physical condition"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent under a unidirectional mind-to-body causal model but lacks bidirectional or multifactorial qualifiers",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "empirical evidence supports measurable psychosomatic pathways, patient finds the framing motivating for cognitive change, and physical symptoms have documented psychological contributors",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "physical illness stems primarily from biomedical factors independent of cognition, or the statement induces self-blame for somatic conditions without addressing organic etiology"
}
